title: The moon followed me home
summary: A child wonders why the moon seems to move with the car on the way home.
body: Last night, my dad drove me home. The road was long. I looked out of the window. The moon was big and white. The car went fast, but the moon was always there. I said, 'Dad, the moon is following us!' My dad smiled. He said, 'The moon is very far. It cannot follow a car.' But I was not sure. I watched the moon for a long time. The trees moved past us. The houses moved past us. The moon did not move past us. When we stopped, the moon stopped too. I went into the house. I looked up at the sky. The moon was still there.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'past simple' cho các hành động đã hoàn thành trong quá khứ: drove, went, said, smiled, watched, stopped.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the past simple for completed actions in the past: drove, went, said, smiled, watched, stopped.

translationVi: Đêm qua, bố lái xe chở tôi về nhà. Con đường rất dài. Tôi nhìn ra ngoài cửa sổ. Mặt trăng to và trắng. Xe chạy nhanh, nhưng mặt trăng luôn ở đó. Tôi nói: 'Bố ơi, mặt trăng đang đi theo chúng ta!' Bố mỉm cười. Bố nói: 'Mặt trăng rất xa. Nó không thể đi theo một chiếc xe.' Nhưng tôi không chắc. Tôi nhìn mặt trăng rất lâu. Cây cối đi qua chúng tôi. Những ngôi nhà đi qua chúng tôi. Mặt trăng không đi qua chúng tôi. Khi chúng tôi dừng lại, mặt trăng cũng dừng lại. Tôi vào nhà. Tôi nhìn lên bầu trời. Mặt trăng vẫn ở đó.

questions.explanation: 'Last night, my dad drove me home.'

questions.prompt: What did the child see in the sky?
What did the child think the moon was doing?
questions.hint: What did the child say to dad?

questions.prompt: What did the dad say about the moon?
Did the child believe the father?
options: No, the child was not sure
questions.hint: Look at the line after the father speaks.

questions.prompt: What things moved past the car?
questions.explanation: 'The trees moved past us. The houses moved past us.'
questions.hint: Find two nouns in the same sentence pattern.

questions.prompt: What happened to the moon when the car stopped?
questions.explanation: 'When we stopped, the moon stopped too.'
questions.hint: Look near the end of the passage.

questions.prompt: Where was the moon at the very end?
What is the main idea of the passage?
options: A child does not yet understand how things look from far away
The dad does not like driving
questions.explanation: The child's confusion is about perception and distance.
questions.hint: Why does the moon 'seem' to follow them?

blanks.explanation: He looked out of the window of the car.
Dad explains that the moon is very far away.
'When we stopped, the moon stopped too.'
He looked up at the sky.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why does the moon 'seem' to follow the car? What other things in life look one way to a child and a different way to an adult — and which view is more 'true'?
